compositional_truth_check.reconciliation_notes: The biome has undergone a total collapse of biological specimens. The registry-defined plants (P1-P4) are no longer present in any recognizable form.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Desiccated, dark, and devoid of organic growth.
biome_observations.surface_debris: Presence of crushed eggshells (calcium amendment) in the central black pot; no signs of fungal or moss activity.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Clean, no biological debris or secondary sprouts.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-4: Initial state shows a single green leaf in the black pot and a white rabbit anchor.
temporal_deltas.t-4_to_t-3: Leaf remains stable; no significant change.
temporal_deltas.t-3_to_t-2: Leaf shows signs of chlorosis and drooping.
temporal_deltas.t-2_to_t-1: Total disappearance of all plant matter; pots are now empty.
temporal_deltas.t-1_to_current: No change; the biome remains in a state of total loss.

visual_health_inference.conclusion: The biome is currently non-viable. The disappearance of all specimens suggests either total senescence, physical removal, or complete decomposition of the plant matter.
visual_health_inference.evidence: Visual evidence confirms the absence of chlorophyll-bearing tissue across all monitored containers.

anomalies: Persistent presence of non-biological anchor (white rabbit) in an empty pot
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a catastrophic loss of the botanical collection. Over the 5-day observation period, the specimens transitioned from a state of minimal health to complete absence. The pots now contain only substrate and inert decorative elements (eggshells, rabbit figurine). There is no evidence of recovery or new growth.
